title: ZAOGA FIF
content:
Zimbabwe Assemblies of God Africa – Forward in Faith (ZAOGA FIF) is a Spirit-filled, Bible-based Pentecostal movement founded in 1960 by Archbishop Dr. Ezekiel Handinawanhu Guti. What began as a small prayer group in Zimbabwe has grown into one of Africa's largest and most dynamic Christian movements, with a presence in over 70 nations across the globe.

Our Founder

Archbishop Ezekiel Guti received a vision from God to reach the world with the gospel of Jesus Christ. His leadership has been marked by unwavering faith, apostolic authority, and a passion for discipleship. Under his guidance, ZAOGA FIF has planted thousands of churches and trained tens of thousands of leaders across Africa, Europe, and beyond.

Vision & Mission

ZAOGA FIF exists to preach the undiluted gospel of Jesus Christ, make disciples of all nations, and build Spirit-filled communities that demonstrate the love and power of God. The movement is committed to prayer, holiness, and the full operation of the gifts of the Holy Spirit.

Global Reach

From its roots in Zimbabwe, ZAOGA FIF has spread to the UK, USA, Canada, Australia, and throughout Africa and Europe. The Easter Conference is one of many gatherings held globally each year where believers come together to be refreshed, equipped, and sent out.

title: About the Speakers
content:
The Easter Conference is honoured to welcome Pastor Douglas Machinge and Pastor Eugenia Machinge as our main conference speakers. Both are AMFCC post-graduates holding diplomas in Biblical Studies and Christian Leadership. They received Jesus at a tender age and served faithfully as elders for 17 years before answering the Lord's call into full-time ministry. They are currently District Pastors at Warren Park District.

Pastor Douglas Machinge

Pastor Douglas is a teacher and exhorter, powerfully used by God in healing and deliverance. His messages are grounded in Scripture and delivered with conviction, drawing believers into deeper encounter with the living God. He is currently studying for a Bachelor's degree in Church Administration and Christian Leadership.

Pastor Eugenia Machinge

Pastor Eugenia is a teacher and a giver – and she teaches giving passionately. Her ministry carries a generosity of spirit that challenges and inspires the body of Christ to walk in the grace of giving. Together, Pastors Douglas and Eugenia are a couple blessed with four children, all growing in the Lord.

Conference Theme

Ministering under the theme “Arise, Shine” (Isaiah 60:1), Pastors Douglas and Eugenia will lead the conference through sessions calling the church into a new season of glory, healing, and wholehearted consecration to God.

title: SCCI
content:
Southgate Christian Center International (SCCI) is the home church and coordinating body of the Easter Conference. Rooted in the ZAOGA FIF movement, SCCI is a vibrant, Spirit-filled congregation committed to worship, discipleship, and community transformation.

Our Church

SCCI gathers believers from diverse backgrounds into one family, united by faith in Jesus Christ. The church is known for its powerful praise and worship, expository preaching, and a culture of prayer that forms the backbone of every ministry and outreach initiative.

Community Outreach

SCCI is deeply committed to serving the local community. From food banks and welfare support to mentorship programmes and pastoral care, the church seeks to demonstrate the love of Christ in practical, tangible ways to all those within reach.

The Easter Conference

The Easter Conference is SCCI's flagship annual gathering, drawing delegates from across the UK and internationally. It is a time of spiritual renewal, fellowship, and activation where believers are equipped and sent back to their homes, churches, and communities refreshed and empowered.
